author: Nathan.fooo
message:
Gate share and view actions by effective permissions (#423)

* Gate share and view actions by effective permissions

* Fix effective permission review issues

* Fix shared view action permissions

* Address React best-practices review findings

- Cache share access details (in-flight dedup + 10s TTL) and remember
  when the v2 endpoint is unsupported to avoid repeated failed requests
- Invalidate the cache after share mutations so refreshes stay fresh
- Derive the invite access level during render instead of clamping in
  an effect
- Consolidate the duplicated current-user access level resolver
- Hoist the CreateSpaceModal initialPage object to a module constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce page title rename request frequency

Raise the title update debounce from 300ms to 3s so slow typing no
longer sends a request per keystroke, and flush pending updates on
unmount so edits survive navigating away within the debounce window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: repair failing CI tests (gotrue prompt param, embedded trash detection) (#421)

Two main-branch CI failures, red since Jun 24:

1. gotrue.test.ts: commit 98125bf9 added prompt=consent to the Google
   OAuth authorize URL without updating the test's expected URL.

2. database-sidebar-deletion Scenario 5: the view API no longer reports
   a trashed ancestor as parent_view_id (returns null), so on a fresh
   mount the embedded database block could not associate itself with its
   trashed container and rendered the live grid instead of the trash
   placeholder. Detect the trashed container by matching the block's
   database_id against trash entries whose extra marks them as database
   containers, which works regardless of whether the parent chain is
   still reachable.

Verified locally: gotrue jest suite 11/11 passed, full
database-sidebar-deletion e2e suite 6/6 passed.

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
test: add BDD coverage for database view tab order after reopening (#420)

Adds a Playwright BDD scenario covering the tab-order bug where new
database views created from the tab bar jumped to the first position
after navigating away and reopening the database: create a grid
database, add Board and Calendar views from the tab bar, rename them,
verify order, reopen, verify order again.

The web client already sends prev_view_id (the container's last child)
when creating a view; the server previously dropped the field and
prepended. Server fix: AppFlowy-Cloud-Premium#786. Also updates the
stale CreateDatabaseViewPayload comment — omitting prev_view_id now
appends on the backend instead of prepending.

Reproduced the reversed order (Third View, Second View, Grid) against
an unfixed server; passes against the fixed server.

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: send row document source when creating row pages (#418)

* fix: send row document source when creating row pages

* Fix favorite view sync

* feat(sync): send before/after Yjs state vectors on collab updates

Attach before_state_vector/after_state_vector (lib0 v1) to outbound collab
Update messages so the server can detect missing updates. Adds proto
fields 5/6 to Update (regenerated messages.js/.d.ts), captures the vectors
from the Yjs transaction in sync-protocol onUpdate, and threads them
through the persistent sync outbox (a merged drain spans the first
record's before-vector to the last record's after-vector). Covered by new
outbox tests. Backward compatible: empty vectors are treated as absent by
the server, so this is inert when the server feature is off.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lmq1LHFqqDTchv9K2bGEKR

* perf(sync): drop after_state_vector from web collab updates

The server never trusts a client-provided after vector (it derives the
post-update state from the update bytes itself), so sending it was wasted
work. Compute and send only before_state_vector: one Y.encodeStateVector
call per update instead of two, and fewer bytes on the wire. Removes the
field from the outbox record and all build sites (immediate/broadcast,
IDB-fallback, merge-drain) and from handleSyncRequest. The Update proto
still declares field 6 to stay aligned with the server wire contract; it
is simply left unset (serializes as absent -> None on the server).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01Lmq1LHFqqDTchv9K2bGEKR

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: connect banner missing WebSocket status on late mount (#407)

* fix: connect banner

* refactor: read WS status via useSyncExternalStore in ConnectBanner

Replace the manual lazy-useState init + useLayoutEffect subscribe/re-read
with useSyncExternalStore. The emitter caches the latest readyState, so the
banner's snapshot can no longer tear from its subscription and the
late-mount case is handled natively rather than via layout-effect timing.
Also drop the unreachable `!eventEmitter` guards (useEventEmitter throws
when the context is missing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: lazy-init eventEmitter ref in AppSyncLayer

useRef keeps only its first value but still evaluates `new EventEmitter()`
on every render. Initialize lazily and route consumers through a narrowed
non-null `eventEmitter` local (added to the relevant effect/memo deps; it's
ref-backed and stable, so no extra runs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: attach sidebar reorder auto-scroll to the scroll container (#391)

The space reorder monitor passed the sidebar's `folder-views` element
(a non-scrolling `flex-1 flex-col`, overflow: visible) to
`autoScrollForElements`. Pragmatic-drag-and-drop logs "Auto scrolling has
been attached to an element that appears not to be scrollable" and skips
it, so dragging a space near the list's top/bottom edge never auto-scrolled.

Resolve the nearest scrollable ancestor (the AFScroller view, overflow:
auto) before attaching, falling back to no auto-scroll when none exists.
Only the sidebar Outline passes `autoScrollElementRef`, so no other
reorderable list is affected.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: center landing-page error card when embedded in app shell (#390)

The "Page not found" and other RecordNotFound error states reuse the
full-page LandingPage component, whose outer container was sized with
`h-screen w-screen`. When rendered embedded in the app content area
(right of the sidebar), `w-screen` (100vw) starts at the content-area's
left edge and overflows the viewport by the sidebar width, so the card
centered ~half the sidebar width too far right instead of in the
available space.

- LandingPage: add a `fitParent` prop that switches the container to
  `h-full w-full` so it fills its parent. Standalone full-page routes
  (InvalidLink, NotFound, ApproveRequest, etc.) keep the original
  viewport sizing — no regression.
- RecordNotFound: route all 12 embedded error states through a small
  `EmbeddedLandingPage` wrapper that always passes `fitParent`
  (matches the existing h-full/w-full pattern in RequestAccessContent).
- warning.svg: nudge the exclamation mark down ~1.5 units so it is
  optically centered within the triangle (was sitting ~7% high).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: anchor slash menu at caret inside empty checklist items (#388)

Typing "/" inside an empty checklist/to-do item opened the slash menu at
the viewport's top-left corner instead of next to the cursor.

The panel position comes from getRangeRect(), which calls
getBoundingClientRect() on the collapsed caret selection. In Chrome, a
collapsed range at an empty text node sitting next to a
contentEditable=false element returns a degenerate {0,0,0,0} rect. A
to-do line hits this exactly: the empty editable leaf sits right after the
checkbox icon and the block placeholder, both contentEditable=false.

Because a zero DOMRect is still a truthy object, the existing fallback
chain never fired and the panel anchored to {top:0,left:0}.

getRangeRect() now detects a degenerate rect and falls back to the nearest
ancestor element that has a real layout box, so the caret position resolves
correctly. This fixes every consumer (slash / mention / page-reference /
paste-as panels and the selection toolbar) at once.

Added a regression test that reproduces the degenerate-rect case and
asserts the fallback resolves the caret position.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message: Fix stale Slate selection after link edits (#389)

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: enforce inherited read-only access for pages in a view-only shared space (#386)

* fix: enforce inherited read-only access for pages in a view-only shared space

Pages inside a private space shared with View-only access carried no
explicit access_level of their own, so getViewReadOnlyStatus treated them
as editable. Resolve the effective access level by walking the ancestor
chain inside the hidden "Shared with me" space and inheriting from the
nearest ancestor that declares an access_level (an explicit child re-share
still overrides the inherited space level).

- Add findSharedAccessLevel() to resolve inherited shared access
- Consolidate getViewReadOnlyStatus traversal: findView already resolves
  views inside the shared space, so drop the redundant findViewInShareWithMe
  walk and its duplicate space lookup
- Cover the inheritance logic with unit tests and assert the read-only
  editor gate end-to-end in the public-to-private BDD scenario

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show "in Trash" for embedded database whose container is trashed

When an embedded database's container page is moved to trash, the block
showed "permanently deleted" instead of "in Trash". The block's viewId is
a child of the container, and once the container is trashed
ViewService.get(viewId) returns 404, so viewMeta becomes null and the
parent id is lost. The trash list only contains the container id (never
the embedded child view id), so the trash match failed and the block was
misclassified as permanently deleted.

Cache the parent id (keyed by viewId) while the view is still reachable
and fall back to it in the trash check when viewMeta is null. Fixes the
consistently-failing database-sidebar-deletion Scenario 1 and Scenario 6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix: render embedded database in version history preview (#385)

Embedded databases inside a document showed an infinite loading spinner
when previewed in Version history. The version-preview <Editor> was not
given `loadView`/`bindViewSync` from the editor context, so the embedded
DatabaseBlock's useDocumentLoader bailed out ("loadView not available")
and the database collab doc never loaded, leaving DatabaseContent stuck
on a perpetual CircularProgress.

Forward `loadView` and `bindViewSync` (already exposed by useAppOperations
and used by the normal ViewModal path) into the preview editor. The
preview shows the database's live data, matching the normal view —
databases are separate collab objects not captured in the document
version snapshot.

Also applies a few React best-practice cleanups in the same file:
- hoist the static MUI Dialog PaperProps out of render
- reuse the existing handleClose callback for Dialog onClose
- drop the redundant handleSetDateFilter wrapper around setDateFilter

Tests:
- unit (DocumentHistoryModal.test.tsx): asserts the preview Editor
  receives loadView/bindViewSync; fails without the fix.
- e2e (version-history-database.spec.ts): signs in to the seeded
  account, opens the seeded page's Version history, and asserts the
  embedded grid renders with rows (not an infinite spinner).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Nathan.fooo
message:
fix(database): preserve cell data when converting field types (#384)

Converting a field type in the grid could hide or drop cell values:
- MultiSelect -> Text made values disappear, and switching back left them
  hidden, because option resolution used the field's CURRENT type. After a
  switch the type-option lookup missed the (retained) source options, so the
  stored option IDs no longer mapped to names.
- Chained switches (A -> B -> A) overwrote the cell's recorded origin type with
  the intermediate hop, so the round-trip misread the data and rendered blank.

Mirror the desktop (flowy-database2) lazy-conversion model:
- resolve select options by the cell's SOURCE field type, not the field's
  current type (cell.parse.ts);
- preserve the TRUE origin type across chained switches and only clear it when
  the cell returns to its written-at type (useSwitchPropertyType);
- clear source_field_type on a genuine cell edit, so an edited cell becomes
  native at the current type (writeCellToRow).

Also close desktop-parity gaps in cell conversion:
- SingleSelect <-> MultiSelect now keeps the option IDs instead of dropping them;
- RichText -> DateTime parses the text into a unix-seconds timestamp;
- Created/LastEditedTime -> DateTime materializes the row timestamp into the cell.

Adds hook-driven tests covering the conversions and a parameterized round-trip
suite (A -> B -> A preserves the displayed value and raw data) mirroring
desktop's real_data_transform_test.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
